description: Matt is a clone of a powerful drug lord and discovers what he was created for.

title: The True Meaning of Smekday
author: Adam Rex
description: When aliens invade Tip sets off on a road trip with a rogue alien named J.Lo to find her mother.

title: The Last Kids on Earth
author: Max Brallier
description: Jack Sullivan barricades himself in a treehouse during a monster apocalypse with junk food and video games.

title: A Boy Called Bat
author: Elana K. Arnold
description: A boy on the autism spectrum desperately wants to keep the baby skunk his vet mother brings home.

title: Zita the Spacegirl
author: Ben Hatke
description: Zita's best friend is sucked through a portal to another planet and she jumps in after him.

title: The Barren Grounds
author: David A. Robertson
description: Two foster kids discover a portal in their bedroom closet leading to a frozen world of talking animals.

title: Interstellar Cinderella
author: Deborah Underwood
description: A girl who loves fixing spaceships repairs the prince's broken ship at the ball.

title: Navigator
author: RJ Barker
description: A boy and a witch sail across a magical sea dodging monsters and unraveling an ancient mystery.

title: The Ranger's Apprentice: The Ruins of Gorlan
author: John Flanagan
description: Will is apprenticed to the mysterious Ranger Halt and trained in stealth tracking and combat.

title: White Fang
author: Jack London
description: A wolf-dog hybrid survives the wild of the Yukon and slowly learns to trust a kind human master.

title: The Indian in the Cupboard
author: Lynne Reid Banks
description: A boy puts a toy figure in a magic cupboard and it comes to life — a tiny real Iroquois man from 1761.

title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
author: Mark Twain
description: Tom Sawyer's mischief along the Mississippi includes witnessing a murder running away and attending his own funeral.

title: Flush
author: Carl Hiaasen
description: Noah's dad sinks a casino boat to expose its illegal dumping — now Noah has to prove his dad was right.

title: Hoot
author: Carl Hiaasen
description: Roy teams up with a mysterious barefoot boy to save tiny burrowing owls from a pancake-house construction project.

title: Swindle
author: Gordon Korman
description: Griffin's valuable baseball card is stolen by a crooked dealer. He assembles a team of specialists for a heist.

title: The Wild Book
author: Margarita Engle
description: A girl with dyslexia in 1920s Cuba finds freedom in the wild book — a notebook she fills with her own words.

title: Shipwreck at the Bottom of the World
author: Jennifer Armstrong
description: The true story of Shackleton's Antarctic expedition — trapped in ice for months the crew must survive.

title: The Sign of the Beaver
author: Elizabeth George Speare
description: Left alone in the Maine wilderness a boy is helped by a Penobscot boy and learns to survive.

title: Mr. Tucket
author: Gary Paulsen
description: Fourteen-year-old Francis is kidnapped by Pawnees on the Oregon Trail and rescued by a mountain man.

title: The River
author: Gary Paulsen
description: Brian returns to the Canadian wilderness with a government psychologist — and must survive again when things go wrong.

title: Rascal
author: Sterling North
description: A boy raises a mischievous pet raccoon through a memorable year in rural Wisconsin.

title: The One and Only Bob
author: Katherine Applegate
description: Bob the dog must find courage during a devastating storm to save his best friend Ivan.

title: A Dog's Way Home
author: W. Bruce Cameron
description: Bella the dog is separated from her owner and travels 400 miles through the Colorado wilderness to get home.

title: Crenshaw
author: Katherine Applegate
description: Jackson's family is about to become homeless again and his imaginary friend — a cat named Crenshaw — reappears.

title: New Kid
author: Jerry Craft
description: Jordan is one of the few kids of color at a prestigious private school and must navigate two worlds.

title: Amal Unbound
author: Aisha Saeed
description: A Pakistani girl is forced into indentured servitude for a corrupt landlord and must find a way to fight back.

title: Other Words for Home
author: Jasmine Warga
description: Jude and her mother flee Syria to America where she must build a new life — told in verse.

title: Prairie Lotus
author: Linda Sue Park
description: Hanna a half-Chinese girl in 1880s Dakota Territory fights for her place in a new town.

title: Allies
author: Alan Gratz
description: D-Day told from multiple perspectives — a soldier a paratrooper a spy a Resistance fighter.

title: The Night Diary
author: Veera Hiranandani
description: Half-Hindu half-Muslim Nisha writes to her dead mother as India is torn apart by Partition in 1947.

title: Ghost Boys
author: Jewell Parker Rhodes
description: After a twelve-year-old Black boy is shot by police his ghost watches the aftermath alongside Emmett Till.
